project.name: Pressure Atlas
project.audience: museum visitors, marine-science students, and public-research supporters

thesis.concept: Physical depth pulls a pale accession catalog into cobalt while a pressure lens exposes how one sample was collected.
thesis.signature: A draggable pressure lens compares natural interpretation with sonar topology over a procedural trench.
thesis.aestheticRisk: The complete page changes material and color with measured depth rather than dividing content into conventional sections.
thesis.restraint: No particles, decorative metrics, floating cards, or post-processing glow.

concepts.name: Transect Tabletop
concepts.signatureInteraction: Drag a lens across one oblique terrain specimen.
concepts.domainAnchor: A bathymetric transect and accession marker determine every visual state.
concepts.conversionHypothesis: Revealing collection evidence at the marker makes opening the full dive feel consequential.
concepts.tradeoff: The restrained single-scene system gives up broad content density.
concepts.rejectionRisk: Reject if the lens reads as decoration rather than a comparison instrument.

concepts.name: Accession Drawer
concepts.signatureInteraction: Pull open a physical sample drawer to reveal collection evidence.
concepts.domainAnchor: Museum accession drawers and specimen labels define the composition.
concepts.conversionHypothesis: A familiar catalog ritual reduces friction for evidence-first visitors.
concepts.tradeoff: The drawer metaphor is less spatially expressive than the trench.
concepts.rejectionRisk: Reject if the interface resembles a generic accordion or card stack.

concepts.name: Sonar Score
concepts.signatureInteraction: A dive route becomes a playable but silent scientific score.
concepts.domainAnchor: Sonar return bands and dive timecodes determine rhythm and geometry.
concepts.conversionHypothesis: A scrubbed signal makes route uncertainty understandable before the archive opens.
concepts.tradeoff: The signal abstraction needs more onboarding and a stronger static fallback.
concepts.rejectionRisk: Reject if it becomes an audiovisual equalizer with no scientific reading.

selectionRationale: Transect Tabletop ties the strongest conversion action to a legible domain object while keeping the static and mobile alternatives credible.

system.geometry: bathymetric surfaces, circular lens, sparse accession pins
system.material: museum paper, mineral terrain, etched instrument marks

architecture.header: bottom depth ruler with active depth and semantic jump links
architecture.heroFrame: full viewport oblique terrain with no card container
architecture.scrollDepth: one pinned terrain scene changes camera, fog, annotation, and palette across four real depth states
architecture.mobileMutation: five explicit depth tabs and an SVG/AVIF terrain strip replace the continuous camera

graphics.justification: A spatial terrain comparison explains bathymetry and collection depth more clearly than cards or a decorative video.

fallbacks.noWebGL: Responsive SVG transect plus semantic accession list.
fallbacks.reducedMotion: Four labeled depth tabs with instant state changes and no camera interpolation.
fallbacks.lowPower: Static AVIF poster and CSS mask lens; the 3D chunk is not requested.
fallbacks.assetFailure: Terrain description, depth table, and CTA remain in the DOM.

motion.entrance: Offer and static poster appear first; the terrain enhances after idle.
motion.nicheMoment: The pressure lens reveals the collection method at the actual marker.
motion.reducedMotionStrategy: Replace transforms and parallax with discrete labeled states.

claims.text: Pressure Atlas is a fictional demonstration archive.

assets.creator: DEMETA showcase
assets.prompt: Fictional translucent abyssal specimen photographed as an accession object; no text or logos.
